title: Digit Sums
description: Given 3 positive integers A, B and C, find how many positive integers less than or equal to A, when expressed in base B, have digits which sum to C.
input: Input will consist of a series of lines, each containing three integers, A, B and C, 2 ≤ B ≤ 100, 1 ≤ A, C ≤ 1,000,000,000. The numbers A, B and C are given in base 10 and are separated by one or more blanks. The input is terminated by a line containing three zeros.
output: Output will be the number of numbers, for each input line (it must be given in base 10).
source: ICPC, Regionals, South Pacific, South Pacific Region, New Zealand Programming Contest, NZPC 2007, hello70825
